1.1: The elder, To Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.
1.2: Beloved, concerning all things I pray you to prosper and to be in good health, just as your soul prospers.
1.3: For I rejoiced exceedingly on the brothers' coming and testifying of your truth, just as you are walking in truth.
1.4: I have no greater joy than these things, that I should hear of my children walking in the truth.
1.5: Beloved, you are doing faithfully whatever you might work for the brothers, and they are strangers,
1.6: who testified of your love before the church, whom you will do well having sent forward worthily of God.
1.7: For they went out on behalf of the Name, receiving nothing from the Gentiles.
1.8: Therefore we ought to support such, so that we may be fellow workers in the truth.
1.9: I did write something to the church; but the one loving to be first among them, Diotrephes, does not welcome us.
1.10: Because of this, if I should come, I will bring to remembrance his works that he is doing, prattling against us with evil words; and not being satisfied with these, neither does he welcome the brothers himself, and he forbids those intending, and he casts them out of the church.
1.11: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. The one doing good is of God; the one doing evil has not seen God.
1.12: Testimony has been given to Demetrius by all, and by the truth itself; and we also testify, and you know that our testimony is true.
1.13: I had many things to write to you, but I do not want to write to you with ink and pen;
1.14: but I hope to see you soon, and we will speak mouth to mouth. Peace to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.
